text: When caring for wounds over extended periods, always:

options.text: Remove dressings and bandages regularly to check the wound.
Clean wounds with antiseptic before applying dressings.
Apply a tourniquet to prevent excessive bleeding.
Check regularly for signs of infection or reduced circulation.

explanation: When managing wounds over extended durations, regular assessment for circulation, sensation, and movement (CSM) is vital. Checking for signs of secondary infection or constriction caused by bandages is essential for effective long-term wound healing and safety.
